problem_statement: A community arts center's reservation system emitted duplicate confirmation messages for a small subset of bookings after a worker rollout. Deployment records claim one source and image revision across the fleet, but retained process evidence is inconsistent and message traces cannot identify the executing process. Decide what can honestly be concluded and what evidence must be added next, using only archived snapshots and offline replay; no patron messages or live worker perturbations are permitted.

observations.statement: The confirmation sink found 47 bookings with two accepted messages sharing a reservation correlation and content hash within 300 milliseconds; 8,913 other bookings had one message.

Reservation R-66120 produced two confirmation-accepted records 112 milliseconds apart with the same correlation, but neither record carries a worker identifier, process start identifier, or loaded-module fingerprint.
observations.provenance: reservation event ledger and provider receipts

observations.statement: A read-only process snapshot from worker-07 during the incident reports source revision src-8f41 and image label img-a91, but the loaded confirmation handler hash is mod-77c rather than mod-b21 listed in that image's signed inventory.
observations.provenance: incident process snapshot and signed image inventory

observations.statement: A snapshot from worker-02 with the same source revision and image label reports the inventoried mod-b21 handler hash and no unexpected overlay entries.
observations.provenance: worker-02 process snapshot and filesystem inventory

observations.statement: Ten thousand offline reservations replayed from the signed src-8f41 build in a freshly extracted img-a91 each emit one confirmation, including all 47 affected input envelopes.
Affected and unaffected reservations have the same queue schema, template fingerprint, tenant settings, and idempotency-key format, and the duplicate pairs span five room types.
observations.provenance: reservation envelope stratification and settings snapshot

observations.statement: Duplicate confirmations ceased after the scheduled fleet restart, but that maintenance also reassigned queue shards and reclaimed local work directories, so the observation cannot separate process image, shard ownership, and local persisted residue.
observations.provenance: maintenance ledger and post-restart sink audit

observations.statement: The incident retention set lacks worker identity on dequeue and send spans, and worker-07's writable-layer archive was not captured, preventing a correlation-level link between mod-77c and any duplicate pair.
observations.provenance: telemetry coverage inventory and incident retention manifest

observations.statement: The build registry verifies that img-a91 was assembled with mod-b21, but it has no record explaining how worker-07 loaded mod-77c or whether that module handled reservation R-66120.
